name: FactSet =FDS Formula Dictionary
description: Canonical FactSet =FDS Excel formula dictionary. Built from the AUTHORITATIVE, confirmed =FDS formula library (price P_, fundamentals FF_, estimates FE_ incl. FE_ESTIMATE/FE_ACTUAL/FE_VALUATION + prebuilt templates, identifiers FG_) plus the full ~599-keyword FactSet Estimates item library (standard + sector-specific). SUPERSEDES the prior OnDemand-manual dictionary (which carried WRONG field names). This is the canonical reference used by the formula generator, the weekly note, and Phase C prep. Excel form: =FDS(symbol_or_cell,"FORMULA_CODE(args)").
source: Online Assistant p.20420 - Changing the Report Layout

placeholders.0: most recent / current period (negative offsets go back in time)

quote_escaping.price_and_fundamentals: nested strings use doubled double-quotes, e.g. ""DATEJ""
quote_escaping.estimates: nested strings use single quotes, e.g. 'MM/DD/YYYY' or ''

price.label: Closing price (single date / spot)
price.notes: Latest close (point-in-time). Date companion: P_DATE(0,,,"DATEJ"). P_PRICE supports split/dividend adjustment args (see FactSet Sidebar).

price_range.label: Price over date range
price_range.notes: Price time series between {SD} start and {ED} end at {FRQ} frequency (most-recent-first comma args inside the parens). Date companion: P_DATE({SD},{ED},{FRQ},"DATEJ").

price_range_ccy.label: Price over range with currency
price_range_ccy.notes: Price series in an explicit reporting currency (e.g. USD).

total_return.label: Compounded total return (ExDate reinvest)
total_return.notes: Compounded total return between dates, dividends reinvested at ex-date.

volume_cum.label: Cumulative volume between dates
volume_cum.notes: Cumulative share volume over the {SD}->{ED} window. (Daily-bar volume in the weekly/price-series workflow uses P_VOLUME_DAY.)

exch_rate.label: Exchange rate
exch_rate.notes: Spot/period exchange rate between two currencies (example EUR->USD).

market_cap.label: Market capitalization (company, current)
market_cap.notes: Current company market capitalization in {CURRENCY} (e.g. P_MARKET_VAL_CO(USD,1)). The trailing 1 selects the current period.

currency.label: Currency (local name)
currency.notes: Local trading/listing currency name. No date arg.

date_eu.label: Date (European format)
date_eu.notes: Most-recent trading date in European day format. No date arg.

ff_com_shs_out.label: Shares Outstanding - Company
ff_com_shs_out.notes: Shares Outstanding - Company. FactSet Fundamentals item. Template form FF_ITEM(ANN,0,,,RF): periodicity options [ANN, QTR, SEMI, LTM, LTM_SEMI, YTD, CAL]; 0 = most recent period (negative offsets go back); RF = Report Filing basis flag. Date companion: FF_FISCAL_DATE(ANN,0,,,RF,"DATEJ").

fe_estimate.label: Consensus estimate (FE_ESTIMATE function)
fe_estimate.notes: FE_ESTIMATE syntax: FE_ESTIMATE(item, statistic, report_basis, estimate_period, sdate, edate, frq, 'CONTEXT'). Statistics: [MEAN, MED, NEST, STDDEV, HIGH, LOW, LAST, OLDEST, UP, DOWN, MARK, TOTAL, UNCHGED, COEFFVAR]. Report basis: ANNUAL=annual (alias ANN); QUARTERLY=quarterly (alias QTR); SEMI=semiannual; CALA=calendarized time-weighted annual; CALQ=calendarized time-weighted quarterly; CAL4=calendarized 4-quarter sum; LTMA=last twelve months time-weighted annual; LTM4_ROLL=last twelve months 4-quarter sum; NTMA=next twelve months time-weighted annual; NTM4_ROLL=next twelve months 4-quarter sum; STMA=second twelve months time-weighted annual; STM4_ROLL=second twelve months 4-quarter sum; SLTMA=second last twelve months time-weighted annual. Append _ROLL for rolling on ANNUAL/QUARTERLY/CALA/CALQ. Estimate period: annual FY1-FY4/forward +n/absolute YYYY; quarterly FQ1-FQ5; LTM/NTM/STM need no period. NTM = next twelve-month forward (sum of next four unreported quarter estimates). Date function: FE_ESTIMATE_DATE(date_type, , report_basis, estimate_period, 'MM/DD/YYYY', 0, , '') (date types: FISCALPERIODEND, DATE). Estimates nest strings use SINGLE quotes (e.g. '' or 'MM/DD/YYYY').

fe_actual.label: Reported actual (FE_ACTUAL function)
fe_actual.notes: FE_ACTUAL syntax: FE_ACTUAL(ACTUAL, item, report_basis, period, , , , ''). Example: FE_ACTUAL(ACTUAL,EPS,ANNUAL,0,,,,''). Date function: FE_ACTUAL_DATE(FISCALPERIODEND,,ANNUAL,0,'MM/DD/YYYY').

fe_valuation_pe_ntm.label: Forward P/E — NTM (FE_VALUATION, native)
fe_valuation_pe_ntm.notes: FE_VALUATION syntax: FE_VALUATION(item, statistic, report_basis, period, ...). Native next-twelve-month consensus P/E (no in-app price/EPS computation needed). Example FY1: FE_VALUATION(PE,MEAN,ANNUAL,+1,0,,,'').

fe_valuation_pe_fy1.label: Forward P/E — FY1 (FE_VALUATION, native)
fe_valuation_pe_fy1.notes: Native FY1 consensus P/E via FE_VALUATION.

est_fy1_eps_mean.label: FY1 Mean EPS (prebuilt FE_ESTIMATE template)
est_fy1_eps_mean.notes: Confirmed prebuilt =FDS Sidebar estimate template. Code: FE_ESTIMATE(EPS,MEAN,ANNUAL,+1,0,,,'').

est_fy1_eps_mean_roll.label: FY1 Mean EPS - Rolling (prebuilt FE_ESTIMATE template)
est_fy1_eps_mean_roll.notes: Confirmed prebuilt =FDS Sidebar estimate template. Code: FE_ESTIMATE(EPS,MEAN,ANNUAL_ROLL,+1,0,,,'').

est_fy2_eps_mean_roll.label: FY2 Mean EPS - Rolling (prebuilt FE_ESTIMATE template)
est_fy2_eps_mean_roll.notes: Confirmed prebuilt =FDS Sidebar estimate template. Code: FE_ESTIMATE(EPS,MEAN,ANNUAL_ROLL,+2,0,,,'').

est_fq1_eps_mean_roll.label: FQ1 Mean EPS - Rolling (prebuilt FE_ESTIMATE template)
est_fq1_eps_mean_roll.notes: Confirmed prebuilt =FDS Sidebar estimate template. Code: FE_ESTIMATE(EPS,MEAN,QUARTERLY_ROLL,+1,0,,,'').

est_fy1_sales_mean_roll.label: FY1 Mean Sales - Rolling (prebuilt FE_ESTIMATE template)
est_fy1_sales_mean_roll.notes: Confirmed prebuilt =FDS Sidebar estimate template. Code: FE_ESTIMATE(SALES,MEAN,ANNUAL_ROLL,+1,0,,,'').

est_ntm_eps_mean.label: NTM EPS - Rolling (prebuilt FE_ESTIMATE template)
est_ntm_eps_mean.notes: Confirmed prebuilt =FDS Sidebar estimate template. Code: FE_ESTIMATE(EPS,MEAN,NTMA,,0,,,'').

est_ltg_mean.label: Long-Term Growth Mean (prebuilt FE_ESTIMATE template)
est_ltg_mean.notes: Confirmed prebuilt =FDS Sidebar estimate template. Code: FE_ESTIMATE(EPS_LTG,MEAN,,,0,,,'').

est_cal_eps_mean.label: Calendarized EPS (prebuilt FE_ESTIMATE template)
est_cal_eps_mean.notes: Confirmed prebuilt =FDS Sidebar estimate template. Code: FE_ESTIMATE(EPS,MEAN,CALA,1CY,0,,,'').

gics_industry.label: GICS industry name (reference)
gics_industry.notes: GICS industry name from the reference library. NOTE: for THIS user's entitlement the verified-working classification fields are FG_FACTSET_SECTOR / FG_FACTSET_IND (the GICS variants returned no data); FG_GICS_INDUSTRY is carried for reference completeness.

factset_sector.label: FactSet sector (verified classification)
factset_sector.notes: Point-in-time FactSet sector classification — the verified-working classification field for this user's entitlement (preferred over GICS).

factset_industry.label: FactSet industry (verified classification)
factset_industry.notes: Point-in-time FactSet industry classification — the verified-working classification field for this user's entitlement (preferred over GICS).

earnings_date_next.label: Next earnings/report date (OPTIONAL event field)
earnings_date_next.notes: OPTIONAL. FactSet Estimates next scheduled earnings/report date; user-tested. Returns a date value. Used for deterministic event-calendar (mechanical-dislocation) tagging.

ex_dividend_date.label: Ex-dividend date (OPTIONAL event field)
ex_dividend_date.notes: OPTIONAL. FactSet Corporate Actions ex-dividend date; user-tested. Returns YYYYMMDD (e.g. 20260526). Inside =FDS(...) the nested string args need DOUBLED double-quotes: =FDS(<ticker>,"FCA_EVENT_DATE(0,""CASH_DIST"",""EXDATE"",""YYYYMMDD"")"). Used for deterministic mechanical-dislocation event tagging.

estimate_item_keywords.description: Use 'keyword' as the item arg in FE_ESTIMATE/FE_ACTUAL/FE_VALUATION. 'norm' is the uppercase normalized form for lookup.

standard.label: Cash Net Income from Continuing Operations

banks.label: Foreign Exchange and Other Trading Revenue

insurance.label: Book Value Per Share - Excl AOCI
Book Value Per Share - Incl AOCI
Present Value of New Business Premium (PVNBP)

cost_curve_pattern.note: FE prefix = Iron; U prefix = Uranium

production_volumes.label: Total Production Semi Soft Coking Coal (t)

mlp.label: Distributable Cash Flow to Limited Partners
Distributable Cash Flow per Unit to Limited Partners

multifinancial.label: Fee-Related Earnings Per Share - After Tax

realized_prices.label: Realized Price - Natural Gas Before Hedges

real_estate.label: EPRA Net Disposal Value (NDV) Per Share
EPRA Net Reinstatement Value (NRV) Per Share
EPRA Net Tangible Assets (NTA) Per Share
Gains/(Losses) on Sale of Real Estate

retailers.label: Net Sales per Retail Sq. Ft.

report_layout_codes.note: Directional codes appear only in the template, never in the output data file.

codes.default: Symbols down a column, request codes on same row as first symbol. No code needed.
codes.^COL: Place before request codes. Symbols across a row; data codes in columns below. Scans down columns.
codes.^ROW: Switches back to symbols-down-a-column after a ^COL section. May alternate any number of times.
codes.^SHEET: Place before requests. Spreads symbols across worksheets; template is sheet 1; symbols occupy same cell on each subsequent sheet.
codes.blank_rows_cols: Allowed in any layout; useful for time-series (e.g. 4 quarters down a column needs 4 blank rows between symbols).

data_quality_flags.issue: Drop-down label in source reads 'EBITA' (typo); keyword and item label are EBITDA.
Drop-down label reads 'EBITDAR' but keyword is EBITDAX.
Item label lost in source (#NUM!); keyword valid. Restored label: Total Production Sulphuric Acid.
Item label lost in source (#NUM!); keyword valid. Restored label: Performance-Related Earnings Per Share.
Appears in both Mining and Oil sectors. Disambiguate by sector context.
Used as 'Funds from Operations' in estimate templates and in Real Estate item list. Same key, sector-context dependent.
Near-duplicate; Mining=TOTAL_PROD, Oil=Total_PRod (mixed case). Normalize to uppercase but preserve original.
Non-uppercase source keywords. FactSet keywords are generally case-insensitive; store normalized uppercase + original.
Oil Upstream Intl op-income vs post-tax labels appear swapped vs the Dom pair. Verify against live FactSet.
